properties.name: Mama Shelter Prague (Meeting Venue)
properties.theme: RTCM SC 104 & SC 134 meetings, 19–21 May 2026
properties.description: Veletržní 1502/20, Prague 7 – Holešovice. Your meeting venue, in the same neighbourhood as EUSPA.

properties.name: EUSPA – EU Agency for the Space Programme
properties.theme: Galileo, EGNOS, Copernicus — modern GNSS operations
properties.description: Janovského 438/2, Prague 7. Manages the Galileo constellation, EGNOS, and Copernicus. ~5 min walk from the meeting venue.

properties.name: National Technical Museum
properties.theme: Kepler instruments, Bürgi sextants, Habermel astrolabes, timekeeping
properties.description: Kostelní 42, Prague 7. Astronomy exhibition with 220+ instruments including Bürgi's and Habermel's originals. Transferred Kepler Museum exhibition. Tue–Sun 9–6, ~250 CZK.

properties.name: Letná Park — historic noon cannon site
properties.theme: Timekeeping — Prague's pre-radio noon signal (1891–1928)
properties.description: A cannon was fired here at noon daily in response to the Klementinum's flag signal. Panoramic views over Old Town. Beer garden under chestnut trees.

properties.name: Prague Astronomical Clock (Orloj)
properties.theme: Celestial computation, timekeeping since 1410
properties.description: Old Town Hall, Staroměstské nám. 1. World's oldest operating astronomical clock. Designed by Jan Šindel, built by Mikuláš of Kadaň. Tower open daily from 9 AM, ~450 CZK.

properties.name: Prague Meridian
properties.theme: Solar time determination, reference meridian
properties.description: Brass strip in Old Town Square pavement near the Marian Column. Marked Prague's noon from 1652 to 1918. Free, always accessible.

properties.name: Berta Fanta's Salon (U Kamenného stolu)
properties.theme: Einstein's Prague social circle, relativity discussions
properties.description: Staroměstské nám. 551/17. Plaque marks the salon where Einstein played violin and discussed physics with Georg Pick, Max Brod, and Franz Kafka.

properties.name: Týn Church — Tycho Brahe's Tomb
properties.theme: Tycho Brahe — observational astronomy, planetary position data
properties.description: Church of Our Lady before Týn. Red marble tombstone near 1st south pillar. Tue–Sat ~10–1, 3–5. Free admission. Enter via passage at Celetná 5.

properties.name: Ernst Mach Memorial Plaque (EPS Historic Site)
properties.theme: Mach's critique of Newtonian mechanics, influence on Einstein
properties.description: Ovocný trh 562/7. Bronze bust and trilingual plaque (2016) marking Mach's physics institute 1867–1879. European Physical Society Historic Site.

properties.name: The Karolinum — Doppler's 1842 presentation
properties.theme: Doppler effect — announced here on 25 May 1842
properties.description: Ovocný trh 3 / Železná 9. Seat of Charles University since 1383. Christian Doppler presented 'On the Colored Light of Double Stars' to 6 people here. Gothic oriel window.

properties.name: Klementinum — Astronomical Tower and Baroque Library
properties.theme: Noon determination, meridian observations, continuous meteorological record since 1775
properties.description: Mariánské nám. 5 / Karlova. Tower where Prague noon was determined (1842–1928). Guided tours ~50 min, ~404 CZK. Book ahead. Not wheelchair accessible.

properties.name: Kepler's House — Karlova 4
properties.theme: Kepler's 1st and 2nd laws of planetary motion derived here (1607–1612)
properties.description: Karlova 188/4. Bronze plaque and courtyard fountain depicting the solar system. Former Kepler Museum (closed 2017; exhibition transferred to NTM).

properties.name: Charles Bridge
properties.theme: Astrological founding (9 July 1357, 5:31 AM), solstice solar alignment
properties.description: Founded on the palindrome 1-3-5-7-9-7-5-3-1 under precise astrological alignment. Summer solstice sunset aligns through the Old Town Tower to St. Vitus Cathedral. Free, always open.

properties.name: Queen Anne's Summer Palace (Belvedere)
properties.theme: Rudolf II's astronomical observatory; Tycho and Kepler observed here
properties.description: Royal Garden, north of Prague Castle. Renaissance palace converted to observatory by Rudolf II. Garden free, daily 10–7 Apr–Oct. Interior only during exhibitions.

properties.name: Nový Svět 1 — Tycho Brahe's First Prague Home
properties.theme: Tycho Brahe's residence upon arrival in Prague (1600)
properties.description: Nový Svět 76/1 ('U Zlatého Noha'). 1901 plaque. Now Aparthotel Tycho de Brahe. Charming quiet street near Prague Castle.

properties.name: Brahe–Kepler Monument, Pohořelec
properties.theme: The collaboration that produced the laws of orbital mechanics
properties.description: Bronze statue by Josef Vajce (1984) depicting Tycho Brahe and Kepler in conversation. Near Gymnázium Jana Keplera. Free, 24/7.

properties.name: Gymnázium Jana Keplera
properties.theme: Named after Kepler; on Keplerova street near his former quarters
properties.description: Parléřova 118/2. One of the Czech Republic's top secondary schools, named after Kepler since 1971.

properties.name: Strahov Monastery Library
properties.theme: Historic astronomical texts, celestial globes, star atlases
properties.description: Strahovské nádvoří 1/132. Theological and Philosophical Halls with 17th-c. Blaeu globes and possible 14th-c. Venetian star atlas. Daily 9–5, ~190 CZK.

properties.name: Štefánik Observatory, Petřín Hill
properties.theme: Public astronomy, named for astronomer M.R. Štefánik
properties.description: Strahovská 205. Three telescope domes, meteorite exhibition. Tue–Sun, afternoons + evening sessions. ~100 CZK. Funicular closed in 2026 — walk up from Strahov or Újezd.

properties.name: Café Louvre — Einstein's regular café
properties.theme: Einstein met Pick and Heinrich here to discuss gravity and light
properties.description: Národní 1987/22 (1st floor). Founded 1902. Also frequented by Kafka and Čapek. Mon–Fri 8 AM – 11:30 PM, Sat–Sun from 9 AM.

properties.name: Doppler Plaque — Karlovo náměstí
properties.theme: Doppler's Prague residence and the former Polytechnic
properties.description: Karlovo nám. 6/20. White marble plaque (1903) on the site of Doppler's former home/laboratory. ČVUT main campus adjacent.

properties.name: Doppler's Family Home — U Obecního Dvora 7
properties.theme: Where Doppler lived 1843–1847 after announcing his principle
properties.description: U Obecního Dvora 7/799, Prague 1. Bronze plaque with relief portrait (2006) by Ellen Jilemnicka. Private building; plaque visible from street.

properties.name: Viničná 7 — Mach's and Einstein's Physics Institute
properties.theme: Mach 1879–1895; Einstein 1911–1912. General relativity work.
properties.description: Viničná 1594/7, Prague 2. Where Einstein wrote on the equivalence principle and gravitational light deflection. Gilded relief in vestibule. Active university building.

properties.name: Einstein's Apartment — Lesnická 7, Smíchov
properties.theme: Einstein's Prague residence 1911–1912
properties.description: Lesnická 1215/7, Prague 5. Plaque with life-size bronze head (1979). Einstein walked from here across Palacký Bridge to Viničná 7 daily.

properties.name: Prague Castle
properties.theme: Rudolf II's court — centre of European astronomy 1583–1612
properties.description: Rudolf II assembled Tycho, Kepler, Bürgi, and Habermel here. Castle grounds free; interior circuits from ~250 CZK. Daily 6 AM – 10 PM (grounds).

properties.name: U Zlatého Tygra (The Golden Tiger)
properties.theme: Nearby refreshment stop — no direct PNT connection
properties.description: Husova 17. Legendary beer hall, unpasteurized Pilsner Urquell. Visited by Havel and Clinton (1994). Opens daily at 3 PM.

properties.name: U Fleků Brewery
properties.theme: Nearby refreshment — Central Europe's oldest brewery (since 1499)
properties.description: Křemencova 11. Dark lager brewed on-site, Gothic/Renaissance halls, brewery museum. Open daily 10 AM – 11 PM.
